Notes: ITAR-free. 80×60 = 4800px. 17µm pitch (older gen). Requires breakout board. Very limited range for surveillance use.
ITAR-free. 19,200px. 12µm pitch. Same breakout boards needed as T1. No radiometric output.
ITAR-free. Same 160×120 as T2 but WITH radiometric output. +400°C scene range. Needs breakout board ($40-50 extra).
Micro 20×20mm carrier. Converts Lepton SPI to CVBS for direct analog VTX connection. Popular DIY solution. 2.3g board weight.
All-in-one kit: Lepton 3.5 + carrier + housing. CVBS to analog VTX. Easiest DIY path. Good community support.
SPI/I2C — no CVBS, not for live FPV. Shutterless design. Low resolution but very cheap. USB-C variant available.
Thermopile (not VOx) = lower sensitivity. 6-pin SPI. Needs dev effort. Tiny. Cheap but marginal for surveillance range.
~49,000 pixels = 2.5× Lepton. CVBS to analog VTX. USB for SBC recording. Excellent value. Lightweight. Low power.
~110,000 pixels = 5.8× Lepton. Still under 21g and <0.7W. CVBS for live feed. USB for SBC. Good pixel per dollar ratio.
~328,000 pixels = 17× Lepton. Same form factor as T8/T9. 18 palettes. NUC/2D/3D NR. CVBS for live VTX feed. USB for SBC.
FPV-branded. Known good community compatibility. 60fps — smoother than 25-50Hz competing modules. Good for search/surveillance.
Well-documented. 18 color palettes. NUC and 2D/3D NR. Both 640 and 384 variants. USB for SBC logging. Rugged temp range.
Factory-direct Chinese. Similar to generic OEM modules. Ships with CVBS adapter. Good value. Brand support uncertain.
UVC plug-and-play — no drivers needed (Linux/Windows/Jetson). Enclosed housing. Higher weight/power. No CVBS — for SBC recording only.
Made in USA. Developer-friendly SDK. 320×240 = 76,800px = 4× Lepton. Not VOx — thermal CMOS. Slower. Lower sensitivity than Chinese VOx at same price.
Best sensitivity (≤30mK). Proven ruggedness. FLIR SDK. Way over budget for this project. Included as benchmark reference.
